title: Simulation and Optimisation

prerequisites.text: Acceptance into Master's programs in Statistics & Data Science, Data Science or Biostatistics, and/or statistical background as deemed sufficient by the Head of Department.

outline: This module is split into three sections: Simulation (Random Number Generation, Monte Carlo Methods, Statistical Analysis of Simulated Data, Variance Reduction, Bootstrap Methods, Markov Chain Monte Carlo); Fundamentals of Linear and Nonlinear Optimization (Unconstrained and Constrained Optimization, Kuhn-Tucker Duality, Convexity, Quadratic Programming, Dynamic Programming, Stochastic Programming); and Stochastic Methods in Optimization ('No Free Lunch' Theorems, Metaheuristics, Random Search, Simulated Annealing, Evolutionary and Genetic Algorithms, Partition Algorithms).
assessment: For each of the three sections: Assignments 50%, Exam 50%. A subminimum of 40% in each of the assignment and exam component is required.
